title: BlackBerry Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Fiscal Year 2026 Results
text: BlackBerry announced strong financial performance for its fiscal year ended February 28, 2026, marking a significant turnaround from previous years. For the fourth quarter, total revenue reached $156.0 million, representing 10% year-over-year growth. The company achieved adjusted EBITDA of $36.1 million, a 71% increase year-over-year. Net income improved to $24.3 million, continuing an eighth consecutive quarter of gains. For the full year, annual revenue was $549.1 million, up 3% from the prior year. The QNX division generated $268.0 million in revenue with 14% growth. Secure Communications achieved $258.9 million in revenue. The company returned to profitability with $53.2 million in net income versus a $79.0 million loss previously. CEO John J. Giamatteo stated that the company has strengthened its fundamentals while continuing to invest in its people, products and platforms. He emphasized that the company is no longer in transition and is positioned as a growth company ready for expansion ahead. QNX achieved Rule of 40 status and expanded its automotive presence to over 275 million embedded vehicles globally. Secure Communications returned to growth driven by digital sovereignty demand.
